what_it_does: Defines a React `ViShape` component that renders the Vi diamond mark as inline SVG: a white diamond polygon with a colored stroke, overlaid by a second diamond filled with a hatched <pattern> (thin diagonal-spaced lines); supports size, an optional color (default --accent-gold var), and an animated flag that pulses the hatch opacity. Exposes window.ViShape.
observations: Generates a per-render unique pattern id via Math.random().toString(36).slice(2,7).
SVG viewBox 0 0 24 24; the diamond is polygon points '12,2 22,12 12,22 2,12'.
Pattern is a 3x3 userSpaceOnUse with a horizontal line strokeWidth 0.4; opacity 0.6 when animated else 0.3.
When animated, an <animate> tweens the line opacity values '0.2;0.7;0.2' over 1.6s, repeatCount indefinite.
Two stacked polygons: first white fill with colored stroke (strokeWidth 1.5, round joins), second filled with url(#id) hatch.
Wrapped in a <span className='vi-shape'> sized by inline width/height and position relative.
standouts: Reuses the deck's signature gold hatch motif inside an entity mark, with an optional animated pulse.
color defaults to a CSS variable, so the mark inherits the design-system token by default.
conventions: Window-global registration (window.ViShape) consistent with the app.
References design tokens via CSS var default (--accent-gold).
Uses .vi-shape class for the wrapper (matches deck.css entity-shape conventions).
concerns: Math.random() pattern id is not collision-proof and is not stable across re-renders (regenerated each render).
5-char random suffix gives a small but nonzero id-collision chance if many instances mount.
notes: The reusable Vi diamond glyph: a hatched diamond mark, token-colored by default, optionally animated; drop <ViShape/> anywhere as logo/chip/button glyph.
questions: Is the per-render random id intentional, or would a stable id (e.g. useId) be preferred to avoid churn?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: header comment 'the Vi diamond mark, reusable as logo / chip / button glyph' is exactly the rendered, parameterized SVG mark.
apparent_themes: Reuse of the signature hatch pattern
intention_signals: Aims to make the Vi diamond a single reusable, parameterized React mark (size/color/animation) so the brand glyph is defined once and dropped in anywhere, carrying the hatch DNA.
novelty: Encodes the brand's diamond-plus-hatch identity as a small parametric SVG component with an opacity-pulse animation option.
contribution: Contributes the canonical reusable Vi diamond glyph component (token-colored, hatch-patterned, optionally animated) that a fused system would keep as the single home for the brand mark.
summary_for_embedding: React ViShape component rendering the Vi diamond brand mark as inline SVG with a diagonal hatch pattern, configurable size/color (default --accent-gold token) and an optional opacity-pulse animation.
intention_for_embedding: To define the Vi diamond brand glyph once as a reusable, parameterized mark usable as logo, chip, or button glyph.
